text.title: django-celery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-celery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs trained on pre-5.4 docs emit transaction.on_commit(lambda: task.delay(...)) boilerplate instead of task.delay_on_commit(...).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both work, but delay_on_commit is cleaner. Standardise on the modern form. Document which version to use in the project AGENTS.md.

failure-mode.description: Agents add bind=True to a task but never call self.retry, silently losing retry capability.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: bind=True is required for self.retry calls and self.request introspection. If neither is needed, omit bind=True.

failure-mode.description: Celery Beat in file-based scheduler mode resets schedule on every container redeploy; some periodic tasks miss their slot.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use django-celery-beat (DatabaseScheduler) or celery-redbeat so schedules survive deploys.

failure-mode.description: Agents use CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=True in tests and ship code that only works in eager mode.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Eager mode hides broker bugs (visibility timeout, serialiser mismatch). Add at least one integration test against a real Redis worker.

failure-mode.description: Flower is wired behind a public ingress without authentication, exposing task payloads.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always require basic-auth or VPN-only access for Flower. Task payloads may contain PII or credentials.
